channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Pets need a place to go during wildfire evacuations. This shelter is trying to help
item.description: Evacuees from fire zones often scramble to find shelter for pets, and some are just found wandering. A shelter in Colorado is busy trying to help.
item.content:encoded: Evacuees from fire zones often scramble to find shelter for pets, and some are just found wandering. A shelter in Colorado is busy trying to help.
item.dc:creator: Molly Cruse

item.title: A federal lawsuit challenges the development of a whites-only community in Arkansas
item.description: A gated community called Return to the Land in the northeastern Ozarks was designed exclusively for white people. A St. Louis realtor is challenging the plans after her application was rejected.
item.content:encoded: A gated community called Return to the Land in the northeastern Ozarks was designed exclusively for white people. A St. Louis realtor is challenging the plans after her application was rejected.
item.dc:creator: Jacqueline Froelich

item.title: Ebola death toll reaches nearly 1,000 as the DRC struggles to contain the virus
item.description: The current Ebola outbreak is eluding officials who are trying to figure out where it's going to go next. The DRC is training 21,000 people to try to help get a grasp on the outbreak.
item.content:encoded: The current Ebola outbreak is eluding officials who are trying to figure out where it's going to go next. The DRC is training 21,000 people to try to help get a grasp on the outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Hegseth appeared on the Hill. It was a rare chance to ask about the Iran war
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified in the Senate on Tuesday. It's the first time since May that anyone has had a chance to ask questions in public about the escalating war in Iran.
item.content:encoded: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified in the Senate on Tuesday. It's the first time since May that anyone has had a chance to ask questions in public about the escalating war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Quil Lawrence

item.title: How to make the complex world of skincare easy
item.description: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Life Kit breaks down what you really need to know about skincare.

item.title: What's behind the push to get more men on testosterone?
item.description: Testosterone is getting a lot of attention lately. The military just announced it will screen service members for low testosterone. Meanwhile, the Trump administration wants to expand access.
item.content:encoded: Testosterone is getting a lot of attention lately. The military just announced it will screen service members for low testosterone. Meanwhile, the Trump administration wants to expand access.
item.dc:creator: Will Stone

item.title: Trump hits Canada with slew of new 50% tariffs set to take effect in 30 days
item.description: President Trump takes aim at Canada with his latest round of tariffs hitting a variety of goods at 50%.
item.content:encoded: President Trump takes aim at Canada with his latest round of tariffs hitting a variety of goods at 50%.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Arizona primaries test Trump's endorsement power
item.description: Arizona is holding its primaries Tuesday. For many GOP voters, the weight of President Trump's endorsement may sway them in choosing which candidate to back in their party's nominating contests.
item.content:encoded: Arizona is holding its primaries Tuesday. For many GOP voters, the weight of President Trump's endorsement may sway them in choosing which candidate to back in their party's nominating contests.
item.dc:creator: Wayne Schutsky

item.title: The best R&B entries in the 2026 Tiny Desk Contest
item.description: Out of thousands of entries, several R&B artists stood out to the judges in the 2026 Tiny Desk Contest.
item.content:encoded: Out of thousands of entries, several R&B artists stood out to the judges in the 2026 Tiny Desk Contest.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Carter

item.title: Colorado wildfire victims return to their homes for the first time
item.description: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.content:encoded: Colorado's biggest wildfire this year has destroyed more than 300 homes. People who lost them are just now being allowed back to their properties.
item.dc:creator: Briana Heaney
